title: Administrative Management and General Management Consulting Services

narrative.executive_view: 541611 is a broad, fragmented market for operating advice, including strategic planning, budgeting, site selection and business-process improvement [S1]. AI can remove a meaningful share of analytical and document-production labor, but a buyer should underwrite only modest retained savings: most boutique firms sell relationship-led, effort-benchmarked work and the data do not establish code-specific pricing power. The screen is investable only for firms with a transferable client franchise, recurring or outcome-based work, and a management bench; otherwise it is a pressured, not durable, terminal-demand case.
narrative.how_ai_changes_work: Management analysts gather and organize information, analyze it, develop solutions, document recommendations and also interview personnel and work with clients [S2]. That makes research, data synthesis, draft reports, workplans and proposal production plausible automation targets, while workshop facilitation, political navigation, selling and accountable judgment remain human-heavy. The role-based V estimate deliberately gives those latter activities no separate credit and treats the broad 4-digit occupational mix as a proxy for this six-digit code.
narrative.value_capture: AI use is already material in professional services: the Federal Reserve's synthesis of Census BTOS puts the sector at about 33% firm adoption, but the question changed in late 2025 from production to any business function [S3]. Faster delivery therefore does not itself prove margin expansion. Project-based consulting faces client procurement and smaller AI-enabled competitors; the retained-savings assumptions are intentionally below one-half and treat outcomes, proprietary data and repeatable managed work as firm-specific exceptions rather than a sector-wide moat.
narrative.adoption_timing: The 33% professional-services rate is an upper-quality anchor for 541611 rather than a direct measure of effective delivery adoption in $1-10M EBITDA consultancies [S3]. I use 3.5 years to 50% effective adoption: workflow tools are easy to trial, but client data controls, quality review, staff redesign and selling a changed delivery model slow the point at which half of work is reliably produced differently. This timing judgment does not use consolidation or owner age.
narrative.consolidation_economics: The supplied dataset estimates 3,853 in-band firms, but target count is only a starting point because client relationships and partner labor can be portable. Current lower-middle-market professional-services ranges are 2.5x-4.5x EBITDA for $1-3M and 4.0x-6.0x for $3-10M, with key-person dependency a stated driver [S4]. A second lower-middle-market guide specifically identifies management consulting as a low-multiple subsector because of key-person risk and project revenue [S5]. The 3.75x buy and 4.25x exit cases assume a buyer improves transferability and recurring revenue; neither is a claim that the code has automatic multiple expansion.
narrative.terminal_demand: Paid demand for independent advice survives where the firm owns executive trust, specialized context, facilitation and accountability, but the traditional analyst-pyramid product is vulnerable. O*NET's task description itself pairs analysis and report preparation with interviewing and consultation [S2], which is why AI-driven delivery efficiency can coexist with lower billable effort. I classify terminal demand as PRESSURED rather than MELTING: the residual product has value, but firms that simply resell generic analysis face a direct substitution and price-deflation risk.
narrative.risks_and_uncertainty: This code includes several advisory types, while surveys and valuation reports are broader professional-services proxies. Owner-age evidence is national rather than consulting-specific: Gallup reports 52.3% of employer businesses owned by people 55 or older, not the requested 60-plus statistic [S6]. The multiple ranges are indicative private-market observations, not closed 541611 deal comps, and the supplied labor-share/role data have vintage and 4-digit-mix limitations. A live deal requires customer concentration, partner-comp normalization, contract assignability, cash-at-close versus earnout, and actual pricing-model diligence.

sources.title: 2022 NAICS Manual
sources.what_it_supports: Official definition and included activities for 541611, including operating advice, strategic planning, site selection and business-process improvement.

sources.title: O*NET OnLine: Management Analysts
sources.what_it_supports: Management-analyst tasks spanning information gathering, analysis, recommendations, reports, interviews, consultation and interpersonal work.

sources.title: Monitoring AI Adoption in the US Economy
sources.what_it_supports: BTOS-based discussion of AI adoption, including about 33% in professional, scientific and technical services and the late-2025 question change.

sources.title: Windsor Drake EBITDA Multiples by Industry: 2026 Data
sources.what_it_supports: Q1 2026 lower-middle-market professional-services EV/adjusted-EBITDA ranges by EBITDA band and key-person-dependency valuation driver.

sources.title: B2B Professional Services Business Acquisitions: ETA Sector Guide
sources.what_it_supports: Lower-middle-market guide describing management consulting as low-multiple because of project-based, key-person-dependent revenue and showing broad 3.0x-7.0x variation by revenue quality.

sources.title: Most Small-Business Owners Lack a Succession Plan
sources.what_it_supports: National employer-business owner age and ownership-transition evidence; it is a disclosed proxy, not a consulting-specific estimate.

labor_share.method: QCEW 2024 private total annual wages at 541611 ($114,205,171,465) x benefits multiplier 1.4294 [ECEC private-industry total comp $46.60/hr / wages $32.60/hr = 1.4294 (March 2026 reference period)] / SUSB 2022 receipts at 541611 ($205,640,681,000). Vintage mismatch: 2024 wages over 2022 receipts, stated. Level: 6-digit. Harmonized to IPS basis ÷1.3407 (median of 65 dual-method codes).

occupations.title: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel

n_band.derivation: margin 0.1565 (margins.json[54]: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%); revenue/employee $260,722 (SUSB 2022 receipts/employment at 541611); class <5: 79,966 firms, avg revenue/firm $344,991 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $53,991 -> out of $1-10M band; class 5-9: 5,880 firms, avg revenue/firm $1,598,754 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $250,205 -> out of $1-10M band; class 10-19: 3,308 firms, avg revenue/firm $3,199,427 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $500,710 -> out of $1-10M band; class 20-99: 2,992 firms, avg revenue/firm $9,214,358 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $1,442,047 -> IN $1-10M band; class 100-499: 861 firms, avg revenue/firm $32,354,620 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $5,063,498 -> IN $1-10M band; class 500+: 507 firms, avg revenue/firm $202,449,895 (class receipts/firms), implied EBITDA $31,683,409 -> out of $1-10M band; n_band = 3,853
n_band.margin_source: Damodaran Jan 2026: Business & Consumer Services EBITDA/Sales 15.65%

ai_replaceable_share.rationale: O*NET establishes a mixed analytical, reporting, interviewing and relationship task set; fractions reflect only the analytically repeatable portion of each supplied role.
caveats: Supplied roles are a 4-digit 5416 proxy, not a 541611 occupational census.
Residual role composition is particularly uncertain.

breakdown_by_role.rationale: Research, analysis and report drafting are repeatable; interviews and consultation remain human.
Administrative preparation automates, but partner leadership and selling do not.

breakdown_by_role.role: Market Research Analysts and Marketing Specialists
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Research and content production automate; client-development judgment remains.

breakdown_by_role.role: Sales Representatives of Services, Except Advertising, Insurance, Financial Services, and Travel
breakdown_by_role.rationale: Proposal and account research automate; relationship selling remains.
Plans, status material and reporting automate; client coordination remains.
Routine scheduling and service administration are strongly automatable.
Operations analysis and documentation are AI-assisted, with execution judgment retained.
Coding assistance is material, but implementation responsibility remains.
Reconciliation and document work automate, with review and accountability retained.
Administrative HR work automates but sensitive advisory judgment remains.
Residual likely mixes junior delivery and administration with relationship-heavy consulting work.

pi_dist.rationale: Project-based advisory and lower-multiple key-person risk imply meaningful client pass-through; specialized outcome work can retain some savings.
caveats: No code-specific fee-model survey was found; this is a bounded judgment.

pi_moat.rationale: Client trust and implementation accountability provide some defense, but project-based personal relationships are portable.
caveats: Firm-level contract and client-concentration data are required for underwriting.

t50_years.rationale: Current sector use is substantial but is not equivalent to half of delivery being effectively redesigned.
caveats: Professional-services sector adoption is a broad proxy and the BTOS wording changed in 2025.

current_adoption_pct.rationale: Federal Reserve's BTOS synthesis reports professional services adoption at about 33%.
caveats: This is sector 54, not 541611 or the target size band, and measures any-business-function use.

historical_analogs.value: Digital workflow tools have historically shifted analytical and reporting work toward fewer hours, but this packet does not claim a quantified consulting analogue.
historical_analogs.rationale: The role tasks and current AI diffusion support directional workflow change, not a measured historical causal estimate.
caveats: No directly comparable historical consulting study was identified.

owners_60plus_pct.rationale: National evidence shows 52.3% of employer businesses are owned by people 55 or older; 30% is a conservative, unverified conversion to 60-plus for this code.
caveats: Not a 541611 or 60-plus observation; do not treat as measured consulting ownership.

succession_shortage_documented.rationale: Gallup documents widespread owner transition uncertainty and large shares planning closure or lacking a long-term plan.
caveats: Evidence is nationwide and includes nonemployers, not a 541611 succession survey.

active_consolidators.rationale: The lower-middle-market guide confirms an active acquisition category but does not count 541611 consolidators; value is a deliberately low screen input.
caveats: Not a measured count of qualifying acquirers or deals.

buy_mult.rationale: Below the 4.0x-6.0x $3-10M professional-services range midpoint to reflect management consulting's key-person/project risk.
caveats: Professional-services range is a proxy, not a closed 541611 transaction database; EBITDA must be normalized for owner compensation and earnouts.

exit_mult.rationale: A half-turn improvement assumes a successfully de-risked platform with stronger recurring or transferable revenue, not market-wide expansion.
caveats: Exit requires demonstrated transferability; no automatic roll-up premium is assumed.

pricing_structure.value: Mixed hourly, daily, fixed-project and selective outcome-based advisory pricing; base case is effort-benchmarked.
pricing_structure.rationale: The code covers broad operating advice while the deal guide highlights project-based revenue; no direct 541611 fee census was found.
caveats: A target's contracts, renewal history and fee basis can reverse the capture conclusion.

cross_checks.uplift_vs_A_consistent: A moderate role-weighted uplift and 3.5-year t50 are consistent with gradual workflow redesign rather than an immediate labor-elimination thesis.

terminal_value.justification: Relationship-led advice, facilitation and accountability survive, but generic analytical output and effort-anchored deliverables are directly exposed to AI-enabled client substitution and repricing.

cross_checks.no_proxy_confirmed: Not confirmed: AI adoption, owner-age and valuation evidence are disclosed sector, national and professional-services proxies rather than exact 541611 observations.
cross_checks.pricing_model_deflation: AI reduces analyst hours; hourly, daily and fixed-project fees may be rescoped unless the firm has outcome pricing, proprietary data or contractual recurring work.

dataset_mismatch.description: Labor share combines 2024 wages with 2022 receipts and roles are from 4-digit 5416, as supplied.
sensitivity: A different six-digit role mix could materially change V.
Partner compensation normalization can change EBITDA and multiples.

market_boundary.selection_basis: Official definition supports a broad code; score is a blended 541611 view with low confidence in firm-level fee mix.

top_uncertain_inputs.score_impact: A ranges from 5.0 to 1.67 across this range.
M is highly sensitive to a real entry multiple and cannot be inferred from broad market ranges.
C changes directly with retained pricing versus pass-through.

reviewer_note: Every cited URL was reopened on 2026-07-21. Multiples are disclosed professional-services/lower-middle-market proxies and are not presented as 541611 transaction comps.
